Package cc.quaternion.potshaper

Class ValidationUtils

java.lang.Object
cc.quaternion.potshaper.ValidationUtils
public final class ValidationUtils extends Object
Provides static utility methods for validating method parameters and state.

This class contains a collection of common validation checks that throw a PotException with a descriptive message when a validation fails. Each method returns the validated value if it passes, allowing for inline usage in assignments and method calls. All methods are designed to be used for precondition checking, typically at the beginning of a method.

Usage Example


 public void processData(String id, byte[] payload, int count) {
     this.id = ValidationUtils.notBlank(id, "id");
     this.payload = ValidationUtils.notEmpty(payload, "payload");
     this.count = ValidationUtils.positive(count, "count");
     // Proceed with processing...
 }
Since:
1.0
Author:
Quaternion8192

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static double
    finite(double value, String paramName)
    Validates that a double value is a finite number (not NaN and not infinite).
    static double
    inRange(double value, double minInclusive, double maxInclusive, String paramName)
    Validates that a double value is a finite number and lies within a specified inclusive range.
    static double
    nonNegative(double value, String paramName)
    Validates that a double value is a finite number and non-negative (greater than or equal to zero).
    static double
    normalizeAngle(double angle)
    Normalizes an angle in degrees to the range [0, 360).
    static String
    notBlank(String value, String paramName)
    Validates that a string is not null, empty, or consists only of whitespace.
    static byte[]
    notEmpty(byte[] value, String paramName)
    Validates that a byte array is not null and has a length greater than zero.
    static <T> Collection<T>
    notEmpty(Collection<T> value, String paramName)
    Validates that a collection is not null and is not empty.
    static <T> T
    notNull(T value, String paramName)
    Validates that an object reference is not null.
    static double
    percentage01(double value, String paramName)
    Validates that a double value represents a percentage in the unit interval [0.0, 1.0].
    static double
    positive(double value, String paramName)
    Validates that a double value is a finite number and positive (greater than zero).
    static int
    positive(int value, String paramName)
    Validates that an integer value is positive (greater than zero).
    static int
    validIndex(int index, int size, String paramName)
    Validates that an integer is a valid index for a collection or array of a given size.
    static int
    validInsertIndex(int index, int size, String paramName)
    Validates that an integer is a valid insertion index for a collection or array of a given size.

  • Method Details

    • notNull

      public static <T> T notNull(T value, String paramName)
      Validates that an object reference is not null.

      If the provided value is null, a PotException is thrown indicating the parameter name and that it cannot be null.

      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the object to validate
      Parameters:
      value - the object reference to check for null
      paramName - the name of the parameter being validated, used in the exception message
      Returns:
      the validated non-null value
      Throws:
      PotException - if value is null
      Since:
      1.0

    • notBlank

      public static String notBlank(String value, String paramName)
      Validates that a string is not null, empty, or consists only of whitespace.

      The check trims the string and verifies the result is not empty. If the value is null or blank after trimming, a PotException is thrown.

      Parameters:
      value - the string to check
      paramName - the name of the parameter being validated, used in the exception message
      Returns:
      the validated non-blank string (the original, untrimmed value)
      Throws:
      PotException - if value is null or blank
      Since:
      1.0

    • notEmpty

      public static byte[] notEmpty(byte[] value, String paramName)
      Validates that a byte array is not null and has a length greater than zero.
      Parameters:
      value - the byte array to check
      paramName - the name of the parameter being validated, used in the exception message
      Returns:
      the validated non-empty byte array
      Throws:
      PotException - if value is null or has zero length
      Since:
      1.0

    • notEmpty

      public static <T> Collection<T> notEmpty(Collection<T> value, String paramName)
      Validates that a collection is not null and is not empty.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of elements in the collection
      Parameters:
      value - the collection to check
      paramName - the name of the parameter being validated, used in the exception message
      Returns:
      the validated non-empty collection
      Throws:
      PotException - if value is null or Collection.isEmpty() returns true
      Since:
      1.0

    • positive

      public static int positive(int value, String paramName)
      Validates that an integer value is positive (greater than zero).
      Parameters:
      value - the integer value to check
      paramName - the name of the parameter being validated, used in the exception message
      Returns:
      the validated positive integer
      Throws:
      PotException - if value is less than or equal to zero
      Since:
      1.0

    • positive

      public static double positive(double value, String paramName)
      Validates that a double value is a finite number and positive (greater than zero).

      This method first calls finite(double, String) to ensure the value is finite.

      Parameters:
      value - the double value to check
      paramName - the name of the parameter being validated, used in the exception message
      Returns:
      the validated finite, positive double
      Throws:
      PotException - if value is not finite or is less than or equal to zero
      Since:
      1.0
      See Also:
      • finite(double, String)

    • nonNegative

      public static double nonNegative(double value, String paramName)
      Validates that a double value is a finite number and non-negative (greater than or equal to zero).

      This method first calls finite(double, String) to ensure the value is finite.

      Parameters:
      value - the double value to check
      paramName - the name of the parameter being validated, used in the exception message
      Returns:
      the validated finite, non-negative double
      Throws:
      PotException - if value is not finite or is less than zero
      Since:
      1.0
      See Also:
      • finite(double, String)

    • inRange

      public static double inRange(double value, double minInclusive, double maxInclusive, String paramName)
      Validates that a double value is a finite number and lies within a specified inclusive range.

      This method first calls finite(double, String) to ensure the value is finite.

      Parameters:
      value - the double value to check
      minInclusive - the minimum allowed value (inclusive)
      maxInclusive - the maximum allowed value (inclusive)
      paramName - the name of the parameter being validated, used in the exception message
      Returns:
      the validated finite double within the specified range
      Throws:
      PotException - if value is not finite or is outside the range [minInclusive, maxInclusive]
      Since:
      1.0
      See Also:
      • finite(double, String)

    • finite

      public static double finite(double value, String paramName)
      Validates that a double value is a finite number (not NaN and not infinite).
      Parameters:
      value - the double value to check
      paramName - the name of the parameter being validated, used in the exception message
      Returns:
      the validated finite double
      Throws:
      PotException - if value is NaN or infinite
      Since:
      1.0

    • validIndex

      public static int validIndex(int index, int size, String paramName)
      Validates that an integer is a valid index for a collection or array of a given size.

      A valid index must satisfy 0 <= index < size. This is suitable for accessing existing elements.

      Parameters:
      index - the index to validate
      size - the size of the collection or array
      paramName - the name of the parameter being validated, used in the exception message
      Returns:
      the validated index
      Throws:
      PotException - if index is less than zero or greater than or equal to size
      Since:
      1.0

    • validInsertIndex

      public static int validInsertIndex(int index, int size, String paramName)
      Validates that an integer is a valid insertion index for a collection or array of a given size.

      A valid insertion index must satisfy 0 <= index <= size. This is suitable for operations that insert a new element at a specific position.

      Parameters:
      index - the insertion index to validate
      size - the current size of the collection or array
      paramName - the name of the parameter being validated, used in the exception message
      Returns:
      the validated insertion index
      Throws:
      PotException - if index is less than zero or greater than size
      Since:
      1.0

    • normalizeAngle

      public static double normalizeAngle(double angle)
      Normalizes an angle in degrees to the range [0, 360).

      The input angle is first validated to be finite via finite(double, String). The normalization is performed by computing the remainder of division by 360.0 and adjusting negative results to be positive.

      Usage Example

      
 double angle = 450.0;
 double normalized = ValidationUtils.normalizeAngle(angle); // Returns 90.0
      Parameters:
      angle - the angle in degrees to normalize
      Returns:
      the normalized angle in the range [0, 360)
      Throws:
      PotException - if angle is not a finite number
      Since:
      1.0
      See Also:
      • finite(double, String)

    • percentage01

      public static double percentage01(double value, String paramName)
      Validates that a double value represents a percentage in the unit interval [0.0, 1.0].

      This is a convenience method that delegates to inRange(double, double, double, String).

      Parameters:
      value - the double value to check
      paramName - the name of the parameter being validated, used in the exception message
      Returns:
      the validated percentage value
      Throws:
      PotException - if value is not finite or is outside the range [0.0, 1.0]
      Since:
      1.0
      See Also:
      • inRange(double, double, double, String)
